phase_summary: Validate that the existing Spec4 codebase builds cleanly, all 7 existing tests pass, and the app launches correctly under the declared stack. Resolve every gap identified in the code review: declare gunicorn as an explicit runtime dependency in pyproject.toml, add a [tool.ruff] section with explicit E and F rules and line-length 88, add a [tool.mypy] section in strict mode, and add major-version upper bounds to all runtime dependencies. This phase establishes a fully spec-compliant, stable baseline before any new feature work begins. No new application features are introduced.

tech_stack_spec.configurations: pyproject.toml: [tool.ruff] section with select = ["E", "F"], line-length = 88, target-version matching Python 3.12; [tool.mypy] section with strict = true, python_version = "3.12"; all runtime dependencies updated to use >=x.y,<(x+1) version bounds; gunicorn added to [project.dependencies]; pytest and pytest-cov remain in [project.optional-dependencies] dev group.

instructions: Run `uv run pytest --tb=short` against the existing test suite and confirm all 7 tests pass before making any changes. Record the passing output as the baseline.
Run `uv run python -m spec4` (or the declared entry point) to confirm the Dash app launches and the home page renders in a browser without errors.

Open pyproject.toml and locate the [project.dependencies] list. For every runtime dependency (dash, dash-mantine-components, dash-iconify, litellm, a2a-sdk, mcp), inspect the currently pinned lower bound and add a major-version upper bound: e.g. `dash>=2.18,<3` becomes the pattern — use the installed version's major as the ceiling. Do NOT change the lower bounds.
Add `gunicorn>=21.2,<22` (or the current installed major) to [project.dependencies] so it is an explicit runtime dependency, not an implicit one.

Add a [tool.ruff] section to pyproject.toml with the following fields: `line-length = 88`, `target-version = "py312"`, and under [tool.ruff.lint]: `select = ["E", "F"]`. Do not add any ignore rules unless a specific existing violation requires it — document any suppression with an inline comment.

Run `uv run ruff check .` and fix all reported E and F violations in the existing source files. Common fixes: unused imports (F401), undefined names (F821), line-length (E501). Do not reformat logic — only fix linter violations.
Run `uv run ruff format .` to apply consistent formatting across the codebase.

Add a [tool.mypy] section to pyproject.toml with: `python_version = "3.12"`, `strict = true`. Add per-module overrides with `ignore_missing_imports = true` for any third-party packages that lack type stubs (dash, dash_mantine_components, dash_iconify, litellm, a2a_sdk, mcp) — list each as a [[tool.mypy.overrides]] entry.

Run `uv run mypy src/` (adjust path to match the package source root). Fix all type errors that mypy strict reports in first-party code. For errors originating purely from untyped third-party libraries already covered by ignore_missing_imports overrides, confirm they are suppressed correctly.
Re-run `uv run pytest --tb=short --cov=spec4 --cov-report=term-missing` and confirm all 7 tests still pass with no regressions after the pyproject.toml and code changes.
Verify the app still launches cleanly after all changes: run the entry point and load the UI in a browser.

risk_assessment.potential_bottlenecks: 1. mypy strict mode on an existing codebase that has never been type-checked will likely surface a significant number of errors — particularly around untyped function arguments, missing return types, and use of `Any` from untyped third-party libraries. 2. Ruff E501 (line too long) violations may be numerous if the codebase has long strings or chained calls. 3. The major-version upper bounds must be set carefully — if a dependency was installed at e.g. litellm 1.x, the bound should be <2, but if it is already at 2.x the bound should be <3. Setting an incorrect upper bound could break resolution.
risk_assessment.mitigation_strategy: For mypy: use [[tool.mypy.overrides]] with ignore_missing_imports = true and, where absolutely necessary, disable_error_codes to suppress errors from untyped third-party call sites — but only as a last resort and always with an inline comment. For Ruff E501: allow the formatter to handle most cases automatically; for unavoidable long strings (e.g. system prompts), add `# noqa: E501` with a comment. For version bounds: run `uv pip show <package>` to read the currently installed version before writing the upper bound.

verification: Run `uv run ruff check . && uv run ruff format --check . && uv run mypy src/ && uv run pytest --tb=short --cov=spec4 --cov-report=term-missing` — all four commands must exit with code 0 and the pytest run must report 7 tests passed with 0 failures.
